content.text:
<user_query>
I have a reference project scaffolded with the same monorepo structure. In the UI package of that reference project, I added several extra components that I want to port to the existing project.@Procertus-ui-experiment/package.json 

The first set of components is within the Sewdn namespace, and I want to port them all. @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/sewdn  To the existing ui package of the current project@portugal/packages/ui 

The second set concerns the app shell and navigation: bar, site navigation, breadcrumb, and page header; I want to port these as well.@Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/app-shell @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/page-header 

Also, the underlying primitive components from Shadcn can all be overridden, since there are several updates within these components to respect some design guidelines. @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/ui @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/Guidelines.mdx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/GradientGuidelines.mdx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/components/spacing-guidelines.mdx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/design-tokens/Motion/Guidelines.mdx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/design-tokens/Shadow/Guidelines.mdx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/design-tokens/Iconography/Guidelines.mdx 

 for the iconography, it is okay to keep using the Lucid icons instead of the other icon set used in the reference project.

I also want to set up the same Tailwind configuration and theming, but instead of porting the entire color schema from the reference project, keep the existing brand colors or theming from the current project. 

Please support switching between dark, light, and system themes.  @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/styles/themes/default.css @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/lib/utils.ts @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/lib/themes.ts @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/src/styles/themes 
</user_query>

Porting UI from Procertus-ui-experiment into portugal's `packages/ui`: exploring both codebases and implementing the port while preserving portugal's brand colors and Lucide icons.

## 01. Why Lucide

Iconography in the PROCERTUS system is not decorative: it is a functional dialect. We use Lucide (lucide-react) as the authoritative symbol set: tree-shakeable React components, consistent stroke-based geometry, and broad coverage for product and documentation surfaces.
input.old_string:
## 01. Why Hugeicons

Iconography in the PROCERTUS system is not decorative: it is a functional dialect. After evaluating numerous libraries, we selected Hugeicons as our authoritative symbol set. It is the only library that mirrors the "Engineered Rigor" of our data-first philosophy.

input.new_string:
Built on a strict grid, Lucide icons share the same blueprint DNA as our 8px Blueprint
        Radius, so symbols feel consistent with the rest of the interface system.
input.old_string:
Built on a strict grid, Hugeicons share the same blueprint DNA as our 8px Blueprint
        Radius, ensuring every symbol feels "manufactured" rather than hand-drawn.

input.new_string:
Single icon family

Use lucide-react only. Import icons as named components (for example Settings, Search) and pass them to primitives such as{" "} SidebarNavIcon or{" "} IconButton. Prefer iconStroke(size) from{" "} @portugal/ui/lib/utils when you need proportional stroke weight outside the sidebar defaults.

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented to mirror the reference app’s shell, nav, breadcrumbs, and panel stack.

### App shell and layout
- **`estate-shell.tsx`** now wraps the app in **`AppPanelsLayout`** → **`ManagementAppShell`** from `@portugal/ui` (replacing `@portugal/ui-lib`’s `AppShell` / `MainContent`).
- **`CloseMobileSidebarOnRouteChange`** uses `useSidebar()` + pathname (with `void pathname` so Biome accepts the dependency).
- **TanStack `Link`** is wrapped as **`RouterNavLink`** (`forwardRef`) so it matches `AppSidebarNavLinkComponent` / header breadcrumbs.

### Navigation and breadcrumbs
- **`shell/nav-config.ts`** defines primary items (`/plots`, `/config`, `/import-export`), secondary **About** (`/about`), **`isPrimaryNavActive`** (plots section stays active on `/plots/...`), and **`breadcrumbTitleForPath`** for the header crumb label.
- Sidebar uses a small **“PT”** workspace tile (no SVG asset).
- Header: **`showNavigation: false`**, **`showSearch: true`**, breadcrumbs **`Workspace` → …** with `NavLink` for the first crumb.
- **`toolbarEnd`** on `AppHeader` (new prop in **`packages/ui/src/components/app-header.tsx`**) holds **ModeToggle**, guest **token login**, and the **role** chip; signed-in users use the **user menu** + **`onSignOut`**.

### Panels + URL persistence
- **`panels/panel-registry.tsx`** registers **`inspector`** and **`help`** (`inspector-panel.tsx`, **`help-panel.tsx`**).
- **`panels/app-panels-layout.tsx`** renders **`PanelsLayout`** with **`panelWidth={520}`** (same idea as the reference).
- **`panels/use-tanstack-panel-persistence.ts`** implements **`PersistenceLayer`** with **`getRouteApi("__root__").useSearch()` / `useNavigate()`** and merges **`panels`**, **`activePanel`**, **`panelProps`** into root search (aligned with `@portugal/ui` sewdn URL persistence).
- **`search/estate-root-search.ts`** + **`routes/__root.tsx`** **`validateSearch: parseEstateRootSearch`** so those query keys are typed and preserved across navigations.

### How to try panels
Example: add **`?panels=help&activePanel=help`** to any in-app URL, or call **`usePanelsContext()`**’s **`openPanel('help')`** / **`openPanel('inspector', { title: '…' })`** from a route when you wire UI triggers.

**Checks run:** `bun run build` for `frontend-web` and `packages/ui`; `biome check` on the touched `frontend-web` paths (+ biome-ignore for the existing theme bootstrap script).

content.text:
Here’s what was changed:

### 1. `packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css`
- Swapped the Google Fonts `@import` from Inter + Manrope to **Figtree** with the full **upright + italic** range: `ital,wght@0,300..900;1,300..900` (variable weights 300–900).
- Set **`--font-sans`** and **`--font-display`** to **`"Figtree", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif`**, so `font-sans` / `font-display` (and anything using those tokens) all use Figtree.

### 2. `apps/frontend-web/src/routes/__root.tsx`
- In the route **`head()`** `links` array, added:
  - `preconnect` → `https://fonts.googleapis.com`
  - `preconnect` → `https://fonts.gstatic.com` with `crossOrigin: "anonymous"`
  - `stylesheet` → the same Figtree CSS2 URL as in globals (via `FIGTREE_FONT_STYLESHEET`), **before** the app `styles.css` link so the face is available when your CSS runs.

### 3. Storybook HTML head
- **`packages/ui/.storybook/preview-head.html`** (new)
- **`packages/ui-lib/.storybook/preview-head.html`** (new)  

Both include the same preconnect + Figtree stylesheet links so preview iframes load the full weight/italic range.

`body` on the app already uses `font-sans`, which now resolves to Figtree. **`bun run build`** for `frontend-web` completes successfully.

**Note:** Fonts are loaded from both the **`@import` in globals** (for anything that only pulls in CSS) and the **`<link>` tags** in the app and Storybook heads. The browser will reuse the same Google Fonts URL from cache; if you ever want a single load path, you can drop the `@import` and rely only on the head links (Storybook would then depend on `preview-head.html`).

content.text:
Here’s what was added and how it maps to `@portugal/domain-shared` plot-scoring types.

### New deal panels (`apps/frontend-web/src/panels/`)

1. **`plot-deal-panel.tsx`** (`PLOT_DEAL_PANEL_TYPE` = `plot-deal`)  
   - Props: `{ plotId }` (URL-safe).  
   - Loads snapshot via **`usePanelSnapshot`** → **`loadSnapshot`**.  
   - Shows **`PlotRecord`** fields (address, region, municipality, price, KO, booleans, notes).  
   - **`computePlotScores`** for **per-profile** scores (`ProfileDefinition` + `PlotScoreResult.profileScores`).  
   - Lists **raw metrics** per **`CriterionDefinition`** (`PlotRawMetrics`).

2. **`profile-deal-panel.tsx`** (`profile-deal`)  
   - Props: `{ profileId }`.  
   - Shows **`ProfileDefinition`** (code, description, id).  
   - Lists **criterion weights** from **`ScoringConfig.profileWeights[profileId]`** (`ProfileWeights` vs criteria).  
   - Short list of linked criteria + measurement basis.

3. **`criterion-deal-panel.tsx`** (`criterion-deal`)  
   - Props: `{ criterionId }`.  
   - Shows **`CriterionDefinition`** (category, measurement basis, description).  
   - **`ScoringRule`** summary via **`format-scoring-rule.ts`** (`scoringRuleSummary` for numeric / qualitative / multi_select).  
   - **Criterion settings**: weight of that criterion **per profile** (`profileWeights[profileId][criterionId]`).

Shared UI: **`deal-panel-chrome.tsx`** (title + close), **`use-panel-snapshot.ts`** (shared `loadSnapshot`).

### Registry

- **`panel-registry.tsx`** registers the three deal panels + **help**; **`inspector-panel`** was removed.

### UI entry points

- **`plots.index.tsx`**: `RankingsTable` with **`onOpenDealPanel`** → `openPanel("plot-deal", { plotId })`.  
- **`packages/ui-lib/.../rankings-table.tsx`**: optional **`onOpenDealPanel?: (plotId: string) => void`** and a **PanelRight** ghost button (stops row click so row still navigates to the plot).  
- **`plots.$plotId.tsx`**: **“Deal panel”** in `PageHeader` actions → same `plot-deal` for the current plot.  
- **`config.tsx`**: **“Profile deal panel”** / **“Criterion deal panel”** (disabled until a profile/criterion is selected) → `profile-deal` / `criterion-deal` with `selectedProfileId` / `selectedCriterionId`.  
- **`help-panel.tsx`**: short note on how to open deal panels.

### Domain alignment

Types and shapes come straight from **`config.ts`** (`ScoringConfig`, `CriterionDefinition`, `ProfileDefinition`, `ScoringRule`, `ProfileWeights`) and **`entities.ts`** (`PlotRecord`, `PlotRawMetrics`) plus **`computePlotScores`** from the package entry.

`frontend-web` **`bun run build`** passes. `ui-lib` **`tsc`** still reports existing issues unrelated to these edits.

content.text:
Here is what was finished:

### 1. `persistence.ts` (React Router)
- Importing `usePanelsLayoutUrlPersistence` and delegating `useUrlQueryPersistence` to it with `location.search` and `commitSearchParams` that calls `navigate` with the merged query string (same behavior as before, single implementation).

### 2. `Panels/index.ts`
- Added `export * from './panels-layout-url-persistence'` so `usePanelsLayoutUrlPersistence`, `PanelsLayoutUrlPersistenceDeps`, and the `PANELS_URL_*` constants are available from `@portugal/ui` / sewdn Panels.

### 3. `use-url-query-detail-panels.ts`
- File-level JSDoc: **PanelsLayout** should use `useUrlQueryPersistence` or `usePanelsLayoutUrlPersistence`; this file remains the legacy **detail panels** + `usePanels` hook.
- Re-exports the URL persistence API from `./panels-layout-url-persistence`.
- Uses `PANELS_URL_TYPES_PARAM` / `PANELS_URL_ACTIVE_PARAM` for the legacy hook’s query keys (no duplicated string literals).

### 4. `use-tanstack-panel-persistence.ts`
- Replaced the hand-rolled `PersistenceLayer` with `usePanelsLayoutUrlPersistence` from `@portugal/ui`.
- **Important:** TanStack Router’s `location.search` is the **parsed** search object, not `?key=…`, so the hook is fed a string built from `rootRouteApi.useSearch()` via `URLSearchParams` (same three fields as `EstateRootSearch`).
- `commitSearchParams` maps those params back with `navigate({ search: (prev) => …, replace: true })` so root `validateSearch` stays in sync.

`bun run --filter @portugal/ui build` and `apps/frontend-web` `bun run build` both succeed.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### 1. `localStorage` overlay — `apps/frontend-web/src/lib/raw-metrics-overlay.ts`
- Key: `portugal-plot-scoring-raw-metrics-overlay`
- **`mergePlotRawMetricsFromOverlay(plotId, bundle)`** — `{ …overlay[plotId], …bundle }` so **bundled / IndexedDB / repo-backed values win** when the same criterion exists in both places.
- **`syncRawMetricsOverlayFromPlots(plots)`** — after each successful `saveSnapshot`, the overlay is **replaced** with the current plots’ `rawMetrics` only (so deleted plots disappear from the overlay too).

### 2. `plot-storage.ts`
- **`hydrateSnapshot`** — applies the overlay on load.
- **`loadSnapshot`** — returns `hydrateSnapshot(decodeSnapshot(stored))` when reading IndexedDB; first-time seed from `loadSeedDatasetFromSource()` is already hydrated (see below).
- **`loadSeedDatasetFromSource`** — returns `hydrateSnapshot(…)` for both seed override and bundled seed.
- **`saveSnapshot`** — after the IndexedDB `put`, calls `syncRawMetricsOverlayFromPlots` in the browser so the overlay stays aligned with what you persist (still **no writes to git-tracked YAML**).
- **`importPortableDataset`** — hydrates before `saveSnapshot` so imports and overlay combine correctly.

Repo YAML files are never written by this path; only IndexedDB + `localStorage` change at runtime.

### 3. Idealista seed YAML (`apps/frontend-web/src/lib/data/plots/idealista-*.yaml`)
Each file now only carries what is **exact from the URL**:
- **`id`** — stable id (e.g. `idealista-33531311` ↔ listing id in the path)
- **`referenceUrl`** — full Idealista URL  
- **`name`** / **`address`** — `""` until you set them from the listing  
- **`rawMetrics: {}`** — no guessed metrics; editors show empty until you fill them (or they come back from overlay + IndexedDB after you save).

### 4. UI polish
- Empty **`name`**: plot detail header, rankings row title, detail panel title, and `MediaHero` **alt** use **`"Plot"`** when `name` is blank.

### Behaviour summary
| Source | Role |
|--------|------|
| Git YAML | Only URL-safe fields + empty metrics as above; not updated by the app. |
| IndexedDB | Working snapshot (metadata + `rawMetrics` you’ve saved in the app). |
| `localStorage` | Same `rawMetrics` mirror on save; on load, merged **under** bundled values so new seed keys win, missing keys are filled from the overlay. |

**Note:** Existing IndexedDB data is unchanged until you **reset the dataset from seed** or clear storage; until then you may still see old metrics from a previous seed. After a seed reload, empty YAML + overlay restores anything you had stored in `localStorage` for the same plot ids.

content.text:
Here is what was completed for the handoff.

### `/plots` bento dashboard (`plots.index.tsx`)
- Removed the profile **dropdown**; each investment profile is a **bento-style tile** (asymmetric `lg` spans: 7 / 5 / full width).
- Per profile: gradient header with **Profile detail** (opens `PROFILE_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE`), two **Thumbnail** placeholders, **MapPlaceholder**, **SegmentedProgress** (category mix), stats (**Marbles** vs `MARBLE_TARGET`, leader score, average score), **RankingsTable** with **weighted score** plus **Σw·s**, **breakdown** `Σr` (sum of ranks across profiles) and **all #** (cross-profile rank from `plotCrossProfileRank`).
- Row click still goes to the full plot route; the panel icon opens **`plot-detail`**.
- **Add plot / import / delete** behavior preserved; page listens for **`SNAPSHOT_CHANGED_EVENT`** so saves from panels refresh the list.

### Helpers & plumbing
- **`plotCrossProfileRank`** in `plot-dashboard-metrics.ts` (rank plots by sum of per-profile ranks).
- **`SNAPSHOT_CHANGED_EVENT`** dispatched from **`saveSnapshot`**; **`usePanelSnapshot`** subscribes and **`useCallback`** reloads; **`DetailPanelShell`** scroll area slightly taller.
- **`profile-marbles.ts`**: mutable `Record` builds + casts for TS; **`defaultSnapshot`** dead code removed from **`plot-storage.ts`** (was unused / lint noise).

### Plot detail panel (`plot-detail-panel.tsx`)
- **Google Maps** link + **iframe** when `location.lat` / `location.lng` exist; otherwise fallback copy + link by address.
- **MediaHero** (no image in schema → placeholder), listing URL, metadata, **profile scores** as buttons → **`profile-detail`** with `profileId`.
- **Raw metrics**: view mode shows **interpreted 1–5**; **Edit raw metrics** / **Save** (admin **`canManageData`**) using **`PlotCriterionEditorCard`** + **`normalizeRawMetricInput`** + **`saveSnapshot`**.

### Profile detail panel (`profile-detail-panel.tsx`)
- Mirrors dashboard widgets: **category SegmentedProgress**, **top 5** `RankingsTable`, **weighted breakdown** table for the **#1 plot** (w, s, w·s), **Σ ranks / cross #** in the table.
- **Edit** / **Save & normalize marbles**: sliders 0…`MARBLE_TARGET`, then **`normalizeAllProfileWeightsToMarbleTarget`** on save so **every profile sums to `MARBLE_TARGET` (117)**.
- **`MutableWeights`** state to satisfy readonly `ProfileWeights` typing.

`bunx biome check --write` passes on the touched files. `tsc` still reports **existing** issues elsewhere in the app (e.g. `Card variant`, `index.tsx` gradient); **plots / panels / profile-marbles / plot-dashboard-metrics** paths are clean in that output.

Here’s what is in place on **`PlotProfilesDashboardPage`** (`apps/frontend-web/src/pages/plot-profiles-dashboard-page.tsx`):

### Plot detail → side panel only
- **`openPlotPanel(plotId)`** calls **`usePanelsContext<typeof panelRegistry>().openPanel(PLOT_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE, { plotId })`** (same pattern as elsewhere; the UI package exposes this as `usePanelsContext`, not a hook literally named `usePanels`).
- **`RankingsTable`** uses **`onOpenDetailPanel={openPlotPanel}`** so the panel icon opens the plot panel.
- Each row’s **`onNavigate`** calls **`openPlotPanel(plot.id)`**, so a row click opens the plot panel instead of routing to **`/plots/$plotId`**.

### Profile detail → side panel
- **`openProfilePanel(profileId)`** calls **`openPanel(PROFILE_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE, { profileId })`**.
- The **gradient profile banner** is **`role="button"`** with **`onClick`** / **`onKeyDown`** (Enter and Space) to open the profile panel; the **Profile detail** **`Button`** uses **`event.stopPropagation()`** so it doesn’t fire the banner handler twice.
- **Modify rules** is next to **All plots** in **`PageHeader` `actions`**.

**Note:** The old **Today / Weekly** `PillToggle` was dropped in this pass; say if you want that wired back into the subtitle.
